basic_details.job_title: WCL Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 444 Mining Sirdar & Electrical Supervisor Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Western Coalfields Limited (WCL), a subsidiary of Coal India Limited
basic_details.post_name: Mining Sirdar, T&S Grade-C; Assistant Foreman (Trainee) (Electrical)/Electrical Supervisor, T&S Grade-C
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Computer Based Test (CBT), Document Verification, Medical Examination
basic_details.short_information: Western Coalfields Limited (WCL), a Miniratna Cat-1 subsidiary of Coal India Limited under the Ministry of Coal, has released Employment Notification No. 2026/69 dated 16.07.2026 for regular appointment to the posts of Mining Sirdar, T&S Grade-C and Assistant Foreman (Trainee) (Electrical)/Electrical Supervisor, T&S Grade-C. There are 444 vacancies in total, for WCL's Underground (UG) and Opencast (OC) mines spread across Maharashtra and Madhya Pradesh. Selection will be through a Computer Based Test (CBT). Online applications open on 20 July 2026 and close on 10 August 2026. This article cove

important_dates.admit_card_date: Will be intimated through official website
important_dates.date of cbt: Will be intimated through official website
important_dates.display of answer keys: Will be intimated through official website
important_dates.objection management window: Will be intimated through official website
important_dates.result_date: Will be intimated through official website

application_fee.details: Applications from UR/OBC(NCL)/EWS candidates without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and will be summarily rejected. The application fee is non-refundable under any circumstances. Any tax or bank charges other than the prescribed fee will be borne by the candidate. Fee must be paid through online mode only; WCL is not responsible if a candidate deposits the fee by any other mode.

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years as on the cut-off date (10.08.2026). Maximum age: 30 years for Unreserved (UR) and EWS candidates as on the cut-off date. | Category Maximum Age Relaxation Beyond Upper Age Limit UR/EWS No relaxation OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years SC/ST 5 years PwBD (UR) 10 years PwBD (OBC-NCL) 13 years PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years Ex-Servicemen (ESM) Actual period of service rendered in Defence Services + 3 years (UR/EWS), 6 years (OBC-NCL), or 8 years (SC/ST), subject to a maximum age of 50 years Departmental Candidates (CIL & subsidiary company employees) No upper age limit | The Date of Birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination/equivalent certificate issued by a Central/State Board only will be accepted; no subsequent request for change will be entertained.

qualification.details: Candidates with other equivalent qualifications approved by the Central Government, or a higher qualification in the respective discipline, are also eligible. For Mining Sirdar, candidates with higher statutory competency certificates issued by DGMS are also eligible. Degree/Postgraduate/Diploma qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time mode (or their equivalent) will not be considered. Since these vacancies are for both UG and OC mines, candidates holding restricted competency certificates are not eligible. All qualifications must be recognized by AICTE/UGC/appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities. | Minimum age: 18 years as on the cut-off date (10.08.2026). Maximum age: 30 years for Unreserved (UR) and EWS candidates as on the cut-off date. | The Date of Birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination/equivalent certificate issued by a Central/State Board only will be accepted; no subsequent request for change will be entertained.

salary_details.details: In addition to basic pay, other allowances such as Dearness Allowance, attendance bonus, special allowance, HRA, transport subsidy, and medical facility are admissible as per NCWA XI provisions. After successful completion of training/probation, further career growth follows the prevailing cadre schemes and rules.

selection_process: Post Maximum Marks Category Minimum Qualifying Marks
Mining Sirdar, T&S Grade-C 100 UR & EWS 50 Marks
OBC (NCL) 45 Marks
SC/ST 40 Marks
Assistant Foreman (Trainee) (Electrical)/Electrical Supervisor, T&S Grade-C 100 UR & EWS 50 Marks
Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT)
followed by document scrutiny/verification and a medical examination.

The CBT consists of 100 questions: Part 1 – General Aptitude (20 questions); Part 2 – Technical (80 questions). Each question carries 1 mark. Duration: 90 minutes. Questions are multiple-choice with four options. There is no negative marking; no marks are awarded for unattempted questions. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi); in case of any discrepancy
the English version is valid and final.
Candidates are shortlisted for document verification based on CBT marks; the zone of consideration is 3 times the number of vacancies in each category. In case of a tie in CBT marks
it is resolved first by Date of Birth (elder candidate ranked higher)
then by alphabetic order of first name as per the Matriculation certificate. A Merit Panel is published on the WCL website after document verification
followed by a provisional offer of appointment based on relative merit and reservation norms. Candidature will be cancelled if a candidate is found ineligible at any stage - during scrutiny
during probation
or even during the course of employment.
Beyond the common General Aptitude section (English
reasoning
quantitative aptitude
data interpretation
and current affairs)

the Mining Sirdar technical syllabus covers: Explosives and Blasting Practice; Board and Pillar development/depillaring methods; Strata control and support systems; Stowing; Opencast working methods; Drifting; Geology; Mine Surveying; Danger of Inundation; Mine Fires and firefighting; Mine Rescue; Mine Ventilation and gases; Duties of Sirdar; Man-riding systems; Winding and shaft safety; Shaft sinking; Coal dust regulations; Accident classification; Face machineries; basic mine electrical systems; and provisions of the Mines Act 1952
Coal Mines Regulations 2017
and related DGMS circulars. Full syllabus is enclosed as Annexure I of the notification.
Beyond the common General Aptitude section
the technical syllabus covers: Basics of Electrical Engineering (Ohm's Law
Kirchhoff's Law
power factor
safety devices); Power generation economics; Electrical Machineries (motors
generators
transformers); Electric Drives and Control (VFDs
PLC
SCADA

DCS); Switchgear and protective devices; Earthing systems (IS-3043); Neutral systems of power supply; Sub-station design and safety; Overhead lines and underground cables; General electrical safety and first aid; relevant legislation (CEA Regulations 2023
Electricity Act 2003
NBC 2016); and on-site emergency management planning. Full syllabus is enclosed as Annexure II of the notification.

syllabus.details: Beyond the common General Aptitude section (English, reasoning, quantitative aptitude, data interpretation, and current affairs), the Mining Sirdar technical syllabus covers: Explosives and Blasting Practice; Board and Pillar development/depillaring methods; Strata control and support systems; Stowing; Opencast working methods; Drifting; Geology; Mine Surveying; Danger of Inundation; Mine Fires and firefighting; Mine Rescue; Mine Ventilation and gases; Duties of Sirdar; Man-riding systems; Winding and shaft safety; Shaft sinking; Coal dust regulations; Accident classification; Face machineries; basic mine electrical systems; and provisions of the Mines Act 1952, Coal Mines Regulations 2017, and related DGMS circulars. Full syllabus is enclosed as Annexure I of the notification.

how_to_apply: Visit www.westerncoal.in > Career > Recruitment > Employment Notification for the posts > Apply Online. Step-by-step instructions are available on the portal.
Read the complete Employment Notification carefully to assess your eligibility before applying.
Fill in all required details and upload the documents listed above.
Pay the application fee of ₹1,180/- online, if applicable to your category.
Review all details carefully before final submission - no changes are allowed once the form is submitted.
Keep a printout of the filled application for future reference.
Direct Link to Apply for WCL Recruitment 2026
A candidate may apply for only one of the two posts; applying for both will lead to cancellation of candidature.
Only applications submitted through the online recruitment portal will be considered.
If a candidate submits multiple applications, only the one with the higher "Application Sequence Number" will be considered; fees paid against one sequence number cannot be adjusted against another.
important_instructions: Test cities are chosen by the candidate at the time of application and cannot be changed later; WCL reserves the right to allocate or change test cities. No relaxation in the minimum qualification and eligibility criteria is admissible for any category of candidate. Qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time mode are not considered. If a valid caste/category certificate is not uploaded, the candidate will be considered as UR; no request for change of category is allowed later. Certificates will be verified from the issuing authority; candidature will be cancelled if any certificate is found unsatisfactory or not genuine at any stage. EWS/SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/ESM certificates must be in Hindi or English. Ex-Servicemen must produce their discharge certificate at document verification. Candidates must maintain an active email ID and mobile number valid for at least the next one year; admit cards are issued only through the official website/recruitment portal. Candidates employed in Government/Semi-Government/PSU/Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection Certificate at document verification and a relieving order at the time of joining, if selected. Selected candidates can be posted at any UG or OC mine of any WCL area and are liable to transfer during their service. Appointment is subject to the candidate being found fit in the Initial Medical Examination as per Company standards. No TA/DA will be paid for the CBT, document verification, or joining. The Court of Jurisdiction for any dispute is the Hon'ble High Court of Bombay – Nagpur Bench. In case of any ambiguity, the English version of the Employment Notification is valid. Using unfair means, impersonation, or misbehaviour during the CBT will lead to disqualification, possible debarment from future WCL selection processes, and criminal prosecution. Furnishing false information leads to termination of service; canvassing in any form leads to disqualification.

content_sections.heading: WCL Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: Vacancies shown are provisional and may increase or decrease at the discretion of WCL management. As per Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment/CIL HQ guidelines, the post of Mining Sirdar does not fall under the identified list of positions for PwBD candidates, so PwBD reservation is not applicable for it. For Assistant Foreman (Trainee) (Electrical)/Electrical Supervisor, PwBD reservation applies to candidates with Hard of Hearing (HH); One Arm/One Leg/Both Legs/Leprosy Cured/Dwarfism/Acid Attack Victim (OA, OL, BL, LC, Dw, AAV); Specific Learning Disability (SLD); or Multiple Disabilities involving these categories.

content_sections.heading: WCL Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates with other equivalent qualifications approved by the Central Government, or a higher qualification in the respective discipline, are also eligible. For Mining Sirdar, candidates with higher statutory competency certificates issued by DGMS are also eligible. Degree/Postgraduate/Diploma qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time mode (or their equivalent) will not be considered. Since these vacancies are for both UG and OC mines, candidates holding restricted competency certificates are not eligible. All qualifications must be recognized by AICTE/UGC/appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities. Minimum age: 18 years as on the cut-off date (10.08.2026). Maximum age: 30 years for Unreserved (UR) and EWS candidates as on the cut-off date. The Date of Birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination/equivalent certificate issued by a Central/State Board only will be accepted; no subsequent request for change will be entertained.

rows.minimum_qualification_eligibility_criter: (a) Valid Mining Sirdar Certificate of Competency issued by DGMS, Valid Gas Testing Certificate, Valid First-Aid Certificate, and 3 years' experience working in Underground Mines; OR (b) Diploma in Mining Engineering from a Government-recognized institute, Valid Overman competency certificate issued by DGMS, Valid Gas Testing Certificate, and Valid First-Aid Certificate.
Diploma (minimum 3-year course) in Electrical Engineering from a Government-recognized institute, and a valid Electrical Supervisory Certificate for Mines issued by the Appropriate Government.

rows.maximum_age_relaxation_beyond_upper_age_: Actual period of service rendered in Defence Services + 3 years (UR/EWS), 6 years (OBC-NCL), or 8 years (SC/ST), subject to a maximum age of 50 years

content_sections.heading: WCL Pay Scale and Training/Probation Period 2026
content_sections.text: In addition to basic pay, other allowances such as Dearness Allowance, attendance bonus, special allowance, HRA, transport subsidy, and medical facility are admissible as per NCWA XI provisions. After successful completion of training/probation, further career growth follows the prevailing cadre schemes and rules.

rows.training_probation_period: 6 months probation from date of joining
2 years training from date of joining

content_sections.heading: WCL Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Applications from UR/OBC(NCL)/EWS candidates without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and will be summarily rejected. The application fee is non-refundable under any circumstances. Any tax or bank charges other than the prescribed fee will be borne by the candidate. Fee must be paid through online mode only; WCL is not responsible if a candidate deposits the fee by any other mode.

content_sections.heading: WCL Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by document scrutiny/verification and a medical examination. The CBT consists of 100 questions: Part 1 – General Aptitude (20 questions); Part 2 – Technical (80 questions). Each question carries 1 mark. Duration: 90 minutes. Questions are multiple-choice with four options. There is no negative marking; no marks are awarded for unattempted questions. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi); in case of any discrepancy, the English version is valid and final. Candidates are shortlisted for document verification based on CBT marks; the zone of consideration is 3 times the number of vacancies in each category. In case of a tie in CBT marks, it is resolved first by Date of Birth (elder candidate ranked higher), then by alphabetic order of first name as per the Matriculation certificate. A Merit Panel is published on the WCL website after document verification, followed by a provisional offer of appointment based on relative merit and reservation norms. Candidature will be cancelled if a candidate is found ineligible at any stage - during scrutiny, during probation, or even during the course of employment. Beyond the common General Aptitude section (English, reasoning, quantitative aptitude, data interpretation, and current affairs), the Mining Sirdar technical syllabus covers: Explosives and Blasting Practice; Board and Pillar development/depillaring methods; Strata control and support systems; Stowing; Opencast working methods; Drifting; Geology; Mine Surveying; Danger of Inundation; Mine Fires and firefighting; Mine Rescue; Mine Ventilation and gases; Duties of Sirdar; Man-riding systems; Winding and shaft safety; Shaft sinking; Coal dust regulations; Accident classification; Face machineries; basic mine electrical systems; and provisions of the Mines Act 1952, Coal Mines Regulations 2017, and related DGMS circulars. Full syllabus is enclosed as Annexure I of the notification. Beyond the common General Aptitude section, the technical syllabus covers: Basics of Electrical Engineering (Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Law, power factor, safety devices); Power generation economics; Electrical Machineries (motors, generators, transformers); Electric Drives and Control (VFDs, PLC, SCADA, DCS); Switchgear and protective devices; Earthing systems (IS-3043); Neutral systems of power supply; Sub-station design and safety; Overhead lines and underground cables; General electrical safety and first aid; relevant legislation (CEA Regulations 2023, Electricity Act 2003, NBC 2016); and on-site emergency management planning. Full syllabus is enclosed as Annexure II of the notification.

content_sections.heading: WCL Recruitment 2026: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: Reservation for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD/ESM follows applicable Government of India Acts, rules, and guidelines in force at the time of finalization of results. SC/ST/OBC(NCL) candidates selected purely on merit (without relaxed standards) are adjusted against Unreserved vacancies, not reserved ones; those selected using relaxed standards are counted against reserved vacancies. ESM/PwBD reservation is horizontal: General category ESM/PwBD are adjusted against UR, OBC-NCL category ESM/PwBD against OBC-NCL vacancies, and SC/ST category ESM/PwBD against SC/ST vacancies. OBC(NCL) candidates must upload a valid Non-Creamy Layer caste certificate and self-declaration; the certificate must confirm the candidate does not fall in the Creamy Layer as on the cut-off date. EWS candidates must submit a valid Income & Asset Certificate based on gross annual income for Financial Year 2025-26, as per DoPT OM No. 36039/1/2019-Estt(Res) dated 31.01.2019. Only persons with not less than 40% benchmark disability are eligible for PwBD relaxation/reservation; the original disability certificate must be submitted at document verification. PwBD candidates may opt for a Scribe facility if desired, per applicable GoI provisions, and must submit a Scribe certificate in the prescribed proforma at the time of reporting for the CBT. Ex-Servicemen who have already secured regular Group C/D Government employment after availing ESM reservation benefits are not eligible for ESM reservation and fee concession again, except under specific self-declaration conditions per DOP&T OM No. 36034/1/2014-Estt.(Res) dated 14.08.2014.

content_sections.heading: WCL Recruitment 2026: Documents Required
content_sections.text: While applying online, candidates need to upload: Scanned recent passport-size colour photograph (not older than 3 weeks, JPG/JPEG format) Scanned signature in black ink (JPG/JPEG format) Certificate/Marksheet of Matriculation/Secondary School Examination/equivalent (Central/State Board) Certificate/Marksheet of Intermediate/Higher Secondary Examination/equivalent (optional) Post-specific documents (see below) Valid OBC(NCL)/SC/ST/PwBD certificate, if applicable, in the prescribed format Valid EWS Income & Asset Certificate, if applicable Non-attachment of required/legible documents will be treated as an incomplete application and rejected outright.

rows.post_specific_documents_required: Valid Mining Sirdar Certificate of Competency (DGMS), Valid Gas Testing Certificate, Valid First-Aid Certificate, 3 years' Underground Mines experience certificate; OR Diploma in Mining Engineering with marksheets, Valid Overman competency certificate (DGMS), Valid Gas Testing Certificate, Valid First-Aid Certificate
Diploma (minimum 3-year course) in Electrical Engineering with marksheets, and a valid Electrical Supervisory Certificate for Mines

No relaxation in the minimum qualification and eligibility criteria is admissible for any category of candidate. Qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time mode are not considered. Candidates employed in Government/Semi-Government/PSU/Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection Certificate at document verification and a relieving order at the time of joining, if selected. Selected candidates can be posted at any UG or OC mine of any WCL area and are liable to transfer during their service. Appointment is subject to the candidate being found fit in the Initial Medical Examination as per Company standards. No TA/DA will be paid for the CBT, document verification, or joining. faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in WCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 444 vacancies in total - 220 for Mining Sirdar, T&S Grade-C and 224 for Assistant Foreman (Trainee) (Electrical)/Electrical Supervisor, T&S Grade-C.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for WCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 10 August 2026 at 11:55 PM. It opened on 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for WCL Mining Sirdar and Electrical Supervisor posts?
faq.answer: Both posts carry an initial basic pay of ₹47,330.25 per month as per NCWA XI, plus allowances such as DA, HRA, attendance bonus, and transport subsidy.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for WCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 30 years for UR/EWS candidates as on the cut-off date (10.08.2026), with relaxation of 3 years for OBC(NCL), 5 years for SC/ST, and additional relaxation for PwBD and Ex-Servicemen candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for WCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: UR/OBC(NCL)/EWS candidates pay ₹1,180/- (₹1,000 + GST); SC/ST/PwBD/ESM/Departmental candidates are exempt from the fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for WCL Mining Sirdar and Electrical Supervisor posts?
faq.answer: Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT) of 100 questions in 90 minutes, followed by document verification and a medical examination.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the WCL CBT?
faq.answer: No, there is no negative marking, and no marks are awarded for unattempted questions.

faq.question: Q8. Can a candidate apply for both posts in WCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. A candidate can apply for only one of the two posts; applying for both will lead to cancellation of candidature.

faq.question: Q9. What is the minimum qualification for WCL Mining Sirdar post?
faq.answer: A valid Mining Sirdar Certificate of Competency (DGMS) with Gas Testing and First-Aid Certificates plus 3 years' underground mining experience, or a Diploma in Mining Engineering with a valid Overman certificate, Gas Testing Certificate, and First-Aid Certificate.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply for WCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only via www.westerncoal.in under Career > Recruitment > Apply Online, between 20 July and 10 August 2026.
